Done. Here's what was implemented:

Files Changed
NEW — @/c:\Users\cclem\Dropbox\Source\Projects-24\Leisure_Life_Interactive\lib\campaigns\media\vision-evaluator.ts

applyVisionEvaluationToCategory(candidates, shipName) — per-category OpenAI vision call using ModelName.GPT_5_HIGH (gpt-5)
Fetches each image server-side as base64 before evaluation (Phase 2)
Controlled suitability tag vocabulary (18 tags) + anti-tag vocabulary (13 tags) — no freeform strings pass through
Disqualifies: categoryFit === 'wrong_category', shipMatch === 'wrong_ship', aiScore < 30
Graceful fallback: fetch failure → drop that candidate; all candidates disqualified → return original batch for heuristic ranking; OpenAI init failure → return original batch (Phase 7)
MODIFIED — @/c:\Users\cclem\Dropbox\Source\Projects-24\Leisure_Life_Interactive\lib\campaigns\media\ship-reference-service.ts

After text pre-filter builds candidateMap, groups by category and runs all 6 categories in parallel via Promise.allSettled
Vision survivors replace the heuristic-only candidates in the map
computeFinalRankScore() blends selectionScore + aiScore*2 / 2 for final ranking
buildCurationFromCandidateAI() maps AI output → AssetRecord.curation (globalPriority, suitabilityTags, antiTags, curatorNotes, approvalState: pending_review) on every imported reference asset
Commit: 0ac1434

Verification (per plan success criteria)
A stateroom image with correct ship metadata but categoryFit === 'wrong_category' is now disqualified before import
Existing exclusion-based iteration remains intact (Phase 6 integration is automatic — vision runs on whatever text-filtered candidates remain after exclusions are applied)
curation fields are populated on every vision-augmented import, ready for /tests/media-generation review controls
